annotations.label: Signal as a function of time

anchor.snippet: a signal is represented as a function of time
deterministic (then one speaks of a deterministic signal)

annotations.label: Stochastic signal as path of a stochastic process

anchor.snippet: a realization of a stochastic process
Analog signal processing is for signals that have not been digitized
Continuous-time signal processing is for signals that vary continuously in time
passing through a linear time-invariant filter/system
is referred to as the impulse response of the system
Discrete-time signal processing is for sampled signals
Analog discrete-time signal processing is a technology based on electronic devices

annotations.label: Discrete-time signal processing as theoretical discipline

anchor.snippet: also refers to a theoretical discipline that establishes a mathematical basis for digital signal processing
Digital signal processing is the processing of digitized discrete-time sampled signals
Nonlinear signal processing involves the analysis and processing of signals produced from nonlinear systems
Polynomial signal processing is a type of non-linear signal processing
Statistical signal processing is an approach which treats signals as stochastic processes
one can model the probability distribution of noise incurred when photographing an image
Graph signal processing generalizes signal processing tasks to signals living on non-Euclidean domains
